slices.summary: Recognized that approval doesn't equal completion — many households drop off between approval and signed contractor scope. Built a 30/60/90-day graduated retention follow-up cadence with named contacts per household, treating approved files as active support cases rather than closed victories. Drafting a one-page standard to codify the process and reduce dependency on personal memory and individual heroics to sustain follow-through.
open_loops: How to prevent the process from becoming neglected paperwork by linking it directly to real decisions and outcomes
Team still views approval as the endpoint; cultural shift from completion to continuity needs active work
The one-page standard needs to be completed this week with practical, plain-language framing
decisions: Establish a 30/60/90-day graduated retention follow-up cadence replacing ad-hoc individual callbacks
Assign one named contact per household to reduce confusion and create clear accountability for follow-through
Draft a one-page standard to codify the new process as an executable framework others can adopt
